programs.name: North Dakota Aging in Community (AIC) Project
programs.what_it_does: The North Dakota State University Extension Service’s AIC project supports older adults in rural areas by addressing service gaps and fostering community connections to enable aging in place. It provides tailored resources, improving confidence, quality of life, and resource access, with significant Medicaid cost savings.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults in rural North Dakota communities, such as Lisbon and western Morton Counties.
programs.whats_unique: State university-led initiative specifically targeting rural aging challenges unique to North Dakota's population shift from rural to urban areas, fostering community integration and saving $308 in Medicaid costs per $1 spent.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Reached 4,270 residents in 2024; 86% felt more confident living at home, 80% reported improved quality of life.[1]

programs.name: Senior Safety Program (ND Assistive)
programs.what_it_does: Provides qualifying North Dakotans with home safety devices and professional setup assistance. Empowers aging individuals' independence and prioritizes their safety for peace of mind.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Qualifying older adults in North Dakota.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific program through ND Assistive offering free home safety devices and setup, tailored to local seniors beyond standard federal assistive tech programs.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free home safety devices and professional installation services.

programs.name: Basic Care Assistance Program (BCAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps qualifying individuals pay for care in licensed basic care facilities, similar to assisted living with room, board, and personal care services. Individuals pay only part of the cost, subsidizing the remainder through state funding.[4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Qualifying older adults needing basic care facility services in North Dakota.
programs.whats_unique: North Dakota-specific state-funded assistance for basic care facilities, a designated care level under state licensing, reducing costs beyond federal Medicaid options.[4][6]
programs.benefit_value: Subsidizes portion of costs for room, board, and care services in licensed facilities.

programs.name: North Dakota State Health Insurance Assistance Program (ND SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free counseling and assistance on Medicare claims, paperwork, and related questions. Helps seniors navigate Medicare effectively through dedicated state support.[7]
programs.who_its_for: North Dakota seniors on Medicare or turning 65.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific SHIP program offering personalized Medicare guidance, as all states have SHIPs but tailored to ND insurance department resources.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free expert assistance on Medicare issues and claims.
